title: Psalms 112
subtitle: Blessed Is the One Who Fears the LORD

heb.paragraph: "Hallelujah! Blessed is the one who fears the LORD. Their righteousness (ṣaddîq) endures forever" (vv.1, 3). The companion to Psalm 111: where 111 describes God’s character, 112 describes the God-fearer’s character. Also an acrostic. The God-fearer: delights in commands (v.1), mighty descendants (v.2), wealth in the house (v.3), light in darkness (v.4), generous and just (v.5).

notes.note: "Blessed is the one who fears the LORD, who finds great delight in his commands. Their children will be mighty in the land." The fear of the LORD produces blessed families across generations.
"Even in darkness light dawns for the upright, for those who are gracious and compassionate and righteous." Light in darkness: the upright are not immune to darkness but they have light within it.

refs.note: The beatitudes describe the blessed person this psalm celebrates.
Being merciful as the Father is merciful parallels the psalm's gracious and compassionate person.
The generous person prospering echoes the psalm's blessing on those who lend freely.

hist.context: Psalm 112 is the companion to Psalm 111, also an acrostic. Where 111 describes God's character, 112 describes the person who fears God. The righteous become like God: gracious, compassionate, generous. Wealth and riches in the house (v.3) and being remembered forever (v.6) present temporal blessings wisdom tradition associates with righteousness.

sections.header: Verses 6–10 — “They Will Have No Fear of Bad News”

heb.paragraph: "They will have no fear (lōʾ yîrāʾ) of bad news; their hearts are steadfast, trusting in the LORD" (v.7). Fearlessness rooted in trust. The righteous are secure (v.6), generous to the poor (v.9), their horn lifted high (v.9). The wicked see this and are grieved; they gnash their teeth (v.10). The contrast: the righteous endure; the wicked’s longings come to nothing.

notes.note: "They will have no fear of bad news; their hearts are steadfast, trusting in the LORD. Their hearts are secure, they will have no fear." The double "no fear": bad news does not destabilise because trust is the foundation.

"They have freely scattered their gifts to the poor; their righteousness endures forever. The wicked will see and be vexed." Paul quotes v.9 in 2 Corinthians 9:9 for Christian generosity. Scattering (giving) is how righteousness endures.

refs.note: Paul quotes verse 9 ('they have freely scattered their gifts to the poor') for Christian generosity.
Storing treasures in heaven develops the psalm's imperishable-righteousness theme.
Perfect love casting out fear contrasts with the wicked's fear the psalm describes.

hist.context: The righteous person's 'heart is secure, having no fear' (v.8) contrasts with the wicked whose desires 'will come to nothing' (v.10). The closing image of the wicked gnashing teeth and wasting away portrays frustration at the righteous person's unshakable security. The psalm presents moral order without acknowledging exceptions like Job.

ppl.name: David
ppl.text: David is not mentioned in Psalm 112. The psalm is anonymous and focuses on the general righteous person who fears the LORD.

ppl.name: God
ppl.text: God is a key figure in Blessed Is the One Who Fears the LORD.

ppl.name: Moses↗ People
ppl.text: Moses stands at the centre of the Pentateuch’s narrative — the one man who spoke with God face to face (Exod 33:11; Num 12:8) and mediated the Sinai covenant to Israel. In Leviticus he receives the priestly legislation; in Numbers he leads, intercedes, and ultimately fails at Meribah (Num 20). The writer of Hebrews calls him "faithful in all God’s house" (Heb 3:5) while distinguishing him from Christ, the Son over the house.

ppl.name: Solomon
ppl.text: Solomon is not mentioned in Psalm 112. The psalm focuses on the anonymous righteous person who fears the LORD.

ppl.name: Abraham
ppl.text: Abraham is not mentioned in Psalm 112. The psalm focuses on the anonymous righteous person who fears the LORD.

ppl.name: Jacob
ppl.text: Jacob is a key figure in Blessed Is the One Who Fears the LORD.

chapter_panels.trans: VersionTranslationNIVEven in darkness light dawns for the upright, for those who are gracious and compassionate and righteous.ESVLight dawns in the darkness for the upright; he is gracious, merciful, and righteous.NIVThey will have no fear of bad news; their hearts are steadfast, trusting in the Lord.ESVHe is not afraid of bad news; his heart is firm, trusting in the LORD.

rec.text: ""Blessed is the one who fears the LORD, who finds great delight in his commands. Their children will be mighty in the land." The fear of the LORD produces blessed families across generations."

rows.label: Light in Darkness — 112:1–5
rows.text: The God-fearer: blessed family, light in darkness, gracious and just.

The companion to Psalm 111: where 111 describes God’s character, 112 describes the God-fearer’s character. Also an acrostic. The God-fearer: delights in commands (v.1), mighty descendants (v.2), wealth in the house (v.3), light in darkness (v.4), generous and just (v.5).

The contrast: the righteous endure; the wicked’s longings come to nothing.

themes.note: Faith peaks: no fear of bad news, steadfast heart. Mercy: scattered gifts to the poor. Companion to Psalm 111.

prayer_prompt: Blessed is the one who fears the LORD — their righteousness endures forever. Consider asking God about your fear of Him — whether it's producing the generosity and righteousness described here.
